tool_name: Industrial Heat Pump COP, Annual Energy Saving & Carbon Payback vs Steam Boiler

inputs.name: Heat Source Temperature (T_source) — Waste Heat / Ground
inputs.note: Waste cooling water ≈ 25–40°C; ground loop ≈ 8–12°C; exhaust air ≈ 20–35°C

inputs.name: Heat Pump Carnot Efficiency Factor (η_Carnot)
inputs.note: Industrial heat pumps: η_Carnot ≈ 0.40–0.55; well-optimised: up to 0.60; VDI 4650 §4

inputs.name: Displaced Natural Gas Boiler Efficiency (η_boiler)
inputs.note: Condensing boiler ≈ 93–95%; conventional ≈ 82–88%; old steam boiler ≈ 75–82%

inputs.name: Natural Gas Carbon Emission Factor
inputs.note: UK BEIS 2024: 0.202 kgCO₂/kWh gas; EU average ≈ 0.198; US EIA ≈ 0.181 kgCO₂/kWh

inputs.name: Grid Electricity Carbon Emission Factor
inputs.note: UK grid 2024: 0.233 kgCO₂e/kWh; EU avg ≈ 0.275; US avg ≈ 0.386; decarbonised grid → < 0.10

formulas: T_H_K = T_delivery_c + 273.15; T_L_K = T_source_c + 273.15; COP_Carnot = T_H_K / (T_H_K - T_L_K)   // [dimensionless] Theoretical (Carnot) COP_H Reference: Carnot COP_H = T_H / (T_H − T_L) — thermodynamic upper bound; EN 14511 §3.2
COP_HP = Carnot_efficiency_fraction * COP_Carnot   // [dimensionless] Actual Heat Pump COP (COP_HP) Reference: COP_act = η_Carnot × COP_Carnot; VDI 4650:2009 §4 — practical Carnot fraction

W_elec_kw = Q_delivery_kw / COP_HP; Q_source_kw = Q_delivery_kw - W_elec_kw   // [kW] Electrical Power Input & Heat Source Extraction Reference: First Law: Q_H = W_elec + Q_source; W = Q_H / COP — ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.4

E_elec_kwh_yr = W_elec_kw * operating_hours_yr; Q_heat_kwh_yr = Q_delivery_kw * operating_hours_yr; E_gas_saved_kwh_yr = Q_heat_kwh_yr / (boiler_efficiency_pct / 100)   // [kWh/yr] Annual Electrical Energy & Gas Equivalent Saved Reference: Annual gas displaced = Q_H / η_boiler; electricity consumed = W × h_op

CO2_gas_saved_t = E_gas_saved_kwh_yr * gas_emission_factor_kg_kwh / 1000; CO2_elec_consumed_t = E_elec_kwh_yr * grid_emission_factor_kg_kwh / 1000; CO2_net_saving_t = CO2_gas_saved_t - CO2_elec_consumed_t   // [tCO₂e/yr] Annual CO₂ Savings Reference: Scope 1 gas avoided minus Scope 2 electricity added — GHG Protocol §5

CI_breakeven_kg_kwh = CO2_gas_saved_t * 1000 / E_elec_kwh_yr   // [kgCO₂/kWh] Break-Even Grid Carbon Intensity (Grid Decarbonisation Threshold) Reference: If grid EF > CI_breakeven, heat pump increases net carbon; below CI_breakeven → net carbon saving

V1.error_msg: Delivery temperature ≤ source temperature + 5 K: heat pump provides no thermodynamic lift — direct heat exchange is appropriate instead

V2.error_msg: COP < 2.0: marginal heat pump benefit — evaluate if Carnot efficiency factor is correct; large temperature lift with low η may not justify capital investment

V3.error_msg: Net carbon saving is negative: current grid carbon intensity is too high — heat pump increases CO₂ vs gas boiler; revisit when grid decarbonises below CI_breakeven

smart_warnings.message: Delivery > 120°C: high-temperature heat pump (HTHP) territory — limited commercial units available; verify refrigerant rating (R245fa, R1234ze(Z)) per ASHRAE 34
Low COP on high-carbon grid: net carbon reduction minimal — prioritise grid decarbonisation timeline in investment decision
Very high Carnot COP: small temperature lift — confirm source availability is reliable year-round; COP may fall significantly in winter if source temperature drops
Large heat source extraction (>70% of delivery): verify source stream capacity and minimum outlet temperature — excessive extraction degrades source quality
Break-even grid intensity > 0.5 kgCO₂/kWh: project is carbon-positive now and in most future grid scenarios — strong decarbonisation case; proceed
